Title: Multiplex Expression Cassette Assembly: A flexible and versatile method for building complex genetic circuits in conventional vectors

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: type VI glandular trichomes on leaf and stem surface
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; mutant sequencing; GC–MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NDPS1 blocks monoterpene accumulation, causing deficiency of β-phellandrene and 2-carene while not affecting sesquiterpenes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NDPS1 was targeted by CRISPR/Cas9 and two homozygous mutants with 4-bp or 1-bp deletions were obtained. 2. ndps1 mutants were deficient in monoterpenes on tomato leaf surfaces. 3. Sesquiterpene accumulation was not affected, supporting a specific role of NDPS1 in monoterpene precursor supply.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Monoterpene deficiency; no effect on sesquiterpenes reported
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under 16 h light/8 h dark at 25°C
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ndps1 mutants can serve as a chassis for heterologous production of high-value terpenoids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene here because the study used genome editing to demonstrate pathway blocking/chassis creation rather than detailed enzymology within the central nutritional product case.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tyrosine to L-DOPA and oxidizes L-DOPA to cyclo-DOPA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor and co-intermediate required for target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food colorant; antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; reactive oxygen species scavenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves and whole transgenic plant tissues showing purple-red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; visible pigment phenotype; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to de novo betanin production in tobacco when co-expressed with DODA, 5GT and ADH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AD1 was assembled as an independent cassette in pBeta. 2. It was transcribed in transgenic tobacco plants. 3. Together with DODA, 5GT and ADH, it enabled betanin accumulation and red coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stable transformation in tobacco grown under greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing betalain/betanin pathway in heterologous crops
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of an alternative multi-cassette strategy versus RUBY polyprotein design.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves L-DOPA to form betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor required for target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food colorant; antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; reactive oxygen species scavenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves and whole transgenic plant tissues showing purple-red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; visible pigment phenotype; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to de novo betanin production in tobacco when co-expressed with CYP76AD1, 5GT and ADH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DODA cassette was included in pBeta. 2. DODA transcripts were detected in transgenic tobacco. 3. The engineered plants accumulated betanin, indicating functional pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stable transformation in tobacco grown under greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for introducing betalain production into heterologous plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports formation of betalamic acid that condenses to betanidin.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates betanidin at the 5-hydroxyl position to produce betanin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant; not explicitly stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food colorant; antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; reactive oxygen species scavenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves and whole transgenic plant tissues showing purple-red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; visible pigment phenotype; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required terminal step for betanin production in engineered tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 5GT was assembled into the pBeta multigene construct. 2. The gene was transcribed in tobacco transformants. 3. Betanin accumulation in red transgenic plants supports successful terminal glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stable transformation in tobacco grown under greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal enzyme for stable betanin engineering in crops
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalyzes the final glycosylation step to the named target metabolite.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyrosine precursor supply for betanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-arogenate to L-tyrosine to enhance precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food colorant; antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; reactive oxygen species scavenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves and whole transgenic plant tissues showing purple-red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; visible pigment phenotype; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances metabolic flux toward tyrosine precursor to support betanin synthesis in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A feedback-insensitive ADHα cassette was added to pBeta to increase tyrosine supply. 2. ADH transcripts were detected in transgenic plants. 3. The multigene construct produced red betanin-accumulating tobacco plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stable transformation in tobacco grown under greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable precursor-boosting gene for stacking with core betalain genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper cites Timoneda et al. 2018 for feedback-insensitive ADHα; source species not explicitly stated.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for thymol and carvacrol branch oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secretory glandular trichomes (inferred from source pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in glandular trichomes than in leaves under CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf glandular trichomes / leaf tissues of transgenic tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable multigene expression; promoter swap to trichome-specific CBTS; qRT-PCR; GC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports thymol/carvacrol synthesis in tobacco and yields increase when expressed in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvTPS2 was included in 35S- and CBTS-driven multigene constructs. 2. 35S-driven expression gave only trace thymol/carvacrol, whereas CBTS-driven trichome expression greatly increased yields. 3. TvTPS2 provides γ-terpinene precursor for downstream oxidation to thymol/carvacrol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S-driven mesophyll expression gave very low yields, likely due to limited GPP supply
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Promoter context (35S vs CBTS); stable transformation in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be deployed with tissue-specific promoters matching precursor-rich glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in separate constructs for thymol and carvacrol depending on CYP71D partner.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated γ-terpinene intermediate leading to thymol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates γ-terpinene at the C-3 position in the thymol branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secretory glandular trichomes (inferred from source pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in glandular trichomes than in leaves under CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves of transgenic tobacco, enhanced by trichome-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable multigene expression; promoter swap to trichome-specific CBTS; qRT-PCR; GC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables thymol branch formation in tobacco, with higher accumulation under trichome-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D179 was co-expressed with TvTPS2 and TvSDR1 in pThymol constructs. 2. CBTS-driven expression increased transcript abundance in trichomes and increased thymol yield. 3. This P450 defines the thymol oxidation branch.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very low thymol under 35S-driven leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Promoter context (35S vs CBTS); stable transformation in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Pair with trichome-specific promoters and TvTPS2/TvSDR1 for thymol engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Position-specific oxidation at C-3 described in pathway scheme.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated γ-terpinene intermediate leading to carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates γ-terpinene at the C-6 position in the carvacrol branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secretory glandular trichomes (inferred from source pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in glandular trichomes than in leaves under CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves of transgenic tobacco, enhanced by trichome-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable multigene expression; promoter swap to trichome-specific CBTS; qRT-PCR; GC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables carvacrol branch formation in tobacco, with higher accumulation under trichome-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D180 was co-expressed with TvTPS2 and TvSDR1 in pCarva constructs. 2. CBTS-driven expression increased transcript abundance in trichomes and increased carvacrol yield. 3. This P450 defines the carvacrol oxidation branch.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very low carvacrol under 35S-driven leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Promoter context (35S vs CBTS); stable transformation in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Pair with trichome-specific promoters and TvTPS2/TvSDR1 for carvacrol engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Position-specific oxidation at C-6 described in pathway scheme.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive; CBTS trichome-specific (TvTPS2/CYP cassettes); TvSDR1 described in constructs as 35S-driven cassette in pThymol-CBTS/pCarva-CBTS
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Allylic ketone intermediate leading to thymol/carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes hydroxylated monoterpene intermediates to allylic ketones that tautomerize to thymol or carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secretory glandular trichomes (inferred from source pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance reported in glandular trichomes than in leaves in engineered lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves of transgenic tobacco, enhanced by trichome-focused pathway expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable multigene expression; qRT-PCR; GC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required downstream oxidation step for thymol/carvacrol production in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvSDR1 was included in both thymol and carvacrol constructs. 2. Expression of the full pathway enabled thymol/carvacrol accumulation, especially with trichome-targeted pathway reconstruction. 3. TvSDR1 catalyzes a late oxidation step needed for product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low product accumulation under 35S pathway configuration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Promoter context and glandular trichome targeting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway enzyme to stack with TvTPS2 and branch-specific CYP71D genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Construct description indicates pSDR-35S used even in CBTS constructs.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter with lac operator, IPTG inducible
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces GGPP for carotenoid biosynthesis from isoprenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A; natural coloring pigment; commercial value in food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Operon assembly; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC quantification of β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to β-carotene biosynthesis in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtE was assembled with crtB, crtI and crtY in pCart. 2. The resulting E. coli strain accumulated β-carotene and appeared yellow. 3. Co-expression with SlDXS2 increased β-carotene yields further.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.1 mM IPTG induction; growth in LB at 28°C after induction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for microbial carotenoid production modules
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source organism not specified in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter with lac operator, IPTG inducible
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses GGPP molecules to produce phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A; natural coloring pigment; commercial value in food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Operon assembly; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC quantification of β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to β-carotene biosynthesis in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtB was assembled in the pCart operon. 2. Expression of the carotenoid enzyme set enabled β-carotene accumulation. 3. β-carotene yield was further enhanced by adding SlDXS2.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.1 mM IPTG induction; growth in LB at 28°C after induction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core committed-step enzyme in β-carotene engineering cassettes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source organism not specified in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter with lac operator, IPTG inducible
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces desaturation steps converting phytoene to lycopene through multiple reactions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A; natural coloring pigment; commercial value in food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Operon assembly; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC quantification of β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to β-carotene biosynthesis in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtI was assembled into the pCart operon. 2. The engineered strain produced β-carotene detectable by color and HPLC. 3. Additional SlDXS2 increased flux and final β-carotene yield.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.1 mM IPTG induction; growth in LB at 28°C after induction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core mid-pathway enzyme in microbial carotenoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source organism not specified in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter with lac operator, IPTG inducible
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant; not explicitly stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A; natural coloring pigment; commercial value in food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Operon assembly; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC quantification of β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces β-carotene in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtY was included in the pCart operon. 2. Its presence enabled final conversion to β-carotene in E. coli. 3. β-carotene production increased substantially when SlDXS2 was added upstream.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.1 mM IPTG induction; growth in LB at 28°C after induction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal enzyme for β-carotene production modules
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source organism not specified in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter with lac operator, IPTG inducible
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway / precursor supply for β-carotene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes rate-limiting formation of DXP from pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Responsible for monoterpenoid biosynthesis specifically in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A; natural coloring pigment; commercial value in food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with crtE/B/I/Y; HPLC quantification; phenotype color comparison; growth measurement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts β-carotene yield from ~20 mg/L to ~65 mg/L without affecting E. coli growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDXS2 was added to the pCart pathway to increase MEP-pathway precursor supply. 2. E. coli expressing pCart+D accumulated more β-carotene and showed orange coloration. 3. β-carotene yield increased to about 65 mg/L without growth penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.1 mM IPTG induction; flask cultivation for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent upstream flux-boosting gene for carotenoid pathway stacking in microbes or plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states DXS is the first and rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway.
